## 2026-05-20 - Terminal Raw Mode Keyboard Traps
**Learning:** When a terminal UI uses `tty.setraw()` to read individual keystrokes, standard interrupt signals (like `SIGINT` from Ctrl-C) are not generated by the terminal driver. Instead, the literal byte `\x03` is passed through. If the UI does not explicitly handle `\x03`, it creates a "keyboard trap" where users cannot exit using standard shortcuts.
**Action:** Always map standard interrupt bytes (e.g., `\x03` for Ctrl-C, `\x04` for Ctrl-D) to the appropriate escape/cancel actions when implementing raw-mode character reading. Update prompt hints to inform users they can use Ctrl-C or Esc to cancel.
